photon_filtering.label: Photon Selection & Filtering
photon_filtering.description: Controls which photons are included in processing. All criteria are applied together (AND logic) — a photon must pass every specified filter to be included. Applied before any algorithm runs.

cnf.description: Photon signal confidence filter. Controls the minimum classification confidence for photons to be included. The default matches ICESat-2 project ATL06 settings.

integer_shorthand.-1: all photons including noise and TEP

cnf.format_notes: Integer as scalar means 'at least this level'. Integer as array means 'exactly these levels'. String array means 'exactly these named levels'.
cnf.interaction_detail: Confidence classification is surface-type-dependent. The same photon receives different confidence scores depending on which surface type (srt) is applied. Changing srt changes which photons survive the cnf filter.

required_pairings.with_atl08_class: Set cnf=0 or lower to let all ATL08-classified photons through. cnf=0 is the docs' recommended value; cnf=-2 (includes TEP candidates) is also valid, per SlideRule's grandmesa_atl03_classification example.
required_pairings.with_atl24: Set cnf=-1 (all photons) to let all ATL24-classified photons through

srt.description: Surface type used for photon classification and geophysical corrections. Dynamic mode uses the ATL03 per-photon surface mask. Forcing a surface type overrides the mask for all photons.
srt.interaction_detail: Forcing srt overrides the per-photon surface mask globally. Use when the automatic mask misclassifies the surface (e.g., small lakes classified as land, ice shelf margins).

required_pairings.with_atl08_class: Set srt=0 (land) to allow all ATL08 photon classifications
required_pairings.with_atl24: Set srt=-1 (dynamic) to allow all ATL24 photon classifications

quality_ph.description: ATL03 photon quality classification filter. Identifies instrumental artifacts. Applied independently of cnf.

atl08_class.description: Filter photons by ATL08 land/vegetation classification. Empty means no ATL08 filtering. Requires ATL08 data to exist for the same granules.

required_pairings.cnf: Set to 0 or lower to disable native ATL03 confidence filtering. The docs recommend cnf=0 (no native filtering); cnf=-2 is valid too and includes TEP candidates, per SlideRule's grandmesa_atl03_classification example. A higher value filters photons before ATL08 classification can run, discarding photons ATL08 might otherwise classify.
required_pairings.srt: Set to 0 (land). ATL08 classification operates on land-surface data only.

atl08_class.implicit_behavior: Setting atl08_class activates the ATL08 classifier on its own; the `atl08_class` column appears in the output whether or not atl08_fields is also requested. atl08_fields (ATL08 land_segments fields) is optional and requires either atl08_class or PhoREAL to be active — it does not itself activate the classifier.

pass_invalid.description: Include photons flagged as invalid by ATL03. Normally these are excluded before any processing.

segment_control.label: Segment Geometry & Surface Fitting
segment_control.description: Controls how photons are grouped into extents (SlideRule's custom-length segments) and how surface fits are computed. SlideRule uses 'extent' to distinguish from ATL03's standard 20m segments.

res.description: Along-track posting resolution. Distance between successive extent centers.
res.interaction_detail: res < len = overlapping extents (correlated). res = len = adjacent (independent samples). res > len = gaps. Default 20/40 gives 2:1 overlap.

len.description: Along-track extent length. The window of photons used for each surface fit.
len.interaction_detail: Longer extents average more photons (smoother, less noise) but lose fine-scale surface features. Must have at least cnt photons within len to produce a valid extent.

ats.description: Along-track spread tolerance. Maximum along-track distance a photon can be from the extent center to be included.

cnt.description: Minimum photon count per extent. Extents with fewer photons are discarded.

maxi.description: Maximum iterations for the iterative surface-fitting algorithm.

sigma_r_max.description: Maximum robust dispersion. Extents with residual spread exceeding this value are rejected as poor fits.

H_min_win.description: Minimum height window for surface fitting. Sets the floor on the vertical search range for the fit algorithm.

dist_in_seg.description: Report along-track distance within each extent. Adds a distance column to output.

beam_track_selection.label: Beam & Track Selection
beam_track_selection.description: Controls which of the 6 ICESat-2 beams and which orbital tracks are processed.

beams.description: Which beams to process. Each pair (gt1l/gt1r etc.) consists of one strong and one weak beam, with assignment depending on spacecraft orientation.

spots.description: Spot numbers to process. Orientation-independent alternative to beam selection. Spots 1, 3, 5 are always strong beams regardless of spacecraft attitude.
spots.note: Only supported by the atl03x endpoint. Other APIs use the beams parameter.

granule_selection.label: Granule Filtering
granule_selection.description: Constrain which HDF5 granules are processed beyond the spatial/temporal query.

granule.description: Filter granules by orbital parameters or date. All fields default to -1 (no filter).

rgt.description: Reference ground track number. ICESat-2 has 1387 unique RGTs.

region.description: Granule region (1-14). Each ATL03 granule covers one of 14 geographic regions.

algorithms.label: Processing Algorithms
algorithms.description: Optional algorithm / configuration blocks. On atl03x, fit / phoreal / yapc enable processing modes that either replace or append to the base photon DataFrame, and atl24 controls integration with the ATL24 bathymetry product. The phoreal block is also read by atl08x as configuration (no algorithm runs there). The atl24 and atl13 blocks are the canonical configurations for atl24x and atl13x respectively. See per-param applies_to for the cross-API surface — the group's applies_to is the union, not a default.

fit.description: ATL06-style iterative surface fitting. Replaces per-photon rows with per-extent fitted elevations. An empty dict {} enables fitting with default segment_control parameters. The fit block can also override maxi, sigma_r_max, and H_min_win independently of the top-level segment_control values.

maxi.description: Override of top-level maxi for the fit algorithm specifically.

sigma_r_max.description: Override of top-level sigma_r_max for the fit algorithm specifically.

H_min_win.description: Override of top-level H_min_win for the fit algorithm specifically.

fit.implicit_behavior: An empty object {} is sufficient to enable surface fitting. No fields are required.

phoreal.description: PhoREAL canopy height configuration block (algorithm developed at University of Texas at Austin). On atl03x: runs as an algorithm — computes vegetation metrics from photon distributions within custom-length extents and replaces per-photon rows with per-extent vegetation statistics. On atl08x: a subset of fields (use_abs_h, can_quality_filter, te_quality_filter) is read as configuration only — atl08x reads the native ATL08 land_segments product directly and does not run the PhoREAL algorithm. See per-field applies_to for which fields are honored where.

binsize.description: Vertical bin size for the photon height histogram used to compute canopy metrics.

geoloc.description: Method for computing the geolocation (lat, lon, along-track distance, time) of each extent.

valid_values.mean: Average across all photons in the extent
valid_values.median: Median value across all photons in the extent
valid_values.center: Halfway point calculated from the average of the first and last photon positions

use_abs_h.description: Use absolute (ellipsoidal) photon/canopy heights instead of heights normalized relative to ground.
use_abs_h.note: On atl03x: switches PhoREAL from ground-relative photon heights to absolute heights for the histogram and derived metrics. On atl08x: swaps the HDF5 source path for h_canopy / h_max_canopy / h_min_canopy / h_mean_canopy / canopy_h_metrics from the relative variants to the *_abs variants under land_segments/canopy/. The output column names do not change — only the values do.

above_classifier.description: Enable the above-ground classifier for canopy/ground separation.

send_waveform.description: Include the binned photon height histogram (waveform) as an array column in the output.

can_quality_filter.description: Canopy quality flag filter threshold. Extents (atl03x) or land_segments (atl08x) below this quality are excluded.
can_quality_filter.note: On atl08x, setting any value also activates reading of /land_segments/canopy/can_quality_score and adds it as the can_quality_score output column.

te_quality_filter.description: Terrain quality flag filter threshold. Extents (atl03x) or land_segments (atl08x) below this quality are excluded.
te_quality_filter.note: On atl08x, setting any value also activates reading of /land_segments/terrain/te_quality_score and adds it as the te_quality_score output column.

phoreal.implicit_behavior: Dual semantics by API. On atl03x, providing a phoreal block enables the PhoREAL algorithm runner: the per-photon DataFrame is replaced by per-extent vegetation statistics, ATL08 land surface classifications are consulted, and noise photons are filtered out automatically. On atl08x, the phoreal block is read purely as configuration — no algorithm runs. Only use_abs_h (HDF5 path swap for canopy columns), can_quality_filter (gates can_quality_score column + per-segment threshold), and te_quality_filter (gates te_quality_score column + per-segment threshold) are honored on atl08x; the other fields (binsize, geoloc, above_classifier, send_waveform) have no effect there. The block's output_effect: "replaces" therefore only applies to atl03x.

yapc.description: Yet Another Photon Classifier. Adds a yapc_score column (0-255) to each photon based on local photon density analysis using k-nearest-neighbors.

score.description: Minimum score threshold. Photons with scores below this are excluded. 0 means add scores to all photons without filtering — use for exploratory analysis.

version.description: YAPC algorithm version. 0 reads the pre-computed classification from the ATL03 granule.
version.note: Versions 1-3 are NOT supported by the atl03x endpoint.

knn.description: K nearest neighbors for density calculation. 0 = automatic selection.

min_knn.description: Minimum KNN value when knn is set to automatic.

win_h.description: Vertical window size for neighbor search.

win_x.description: Horizontal (along-track) window size for neighbor search.

atl24.description: Bathymetry photon classification block. On atl24x: the canonical configuration for the endpoint — controls which classified photon types are returned plus quality filters. On atl03x: enables ATL24 integration via the SurfaceBlanket runner — class_ph and confidence_threshold filter ATL24-classified photons that get joined back into the ATL03 photon DataFrame; the other fields (compact, night, low_confidence, sensor_depth_exceeded, invalid_kd, invalid_wind_speed, anc_fields) are honored by atl24x only.

anc_fields.description: Ancillary fields to include from ATL24 bathymetry data.

class_ph.description: Which classified photon types to return in the output.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum classification confidence score. 0 returns all classified photons regardless of confidence.

night.description: Filter by day/night. Default includes both.
night.note: Night passes typically have less solar background noise, producing cleaner bathymetric classifications.

sensor_depth_exceeded.description: Include photons where estimated depth exceeds the sensor's detection capability.

invalid_kd.description: Include photons with invalid diffuse attenuation coefficient (Kd) data.

invalid_wind_speed.description: Include photons with invalid wind speed data.

required_pairings.cnf: Set to -1 (all photons) — bathymetric returns are inherently low-confidence

atl13.description: Inland water body configuration for the atl13x API. Identifies the target water body by name, reference ID, or coordinate.

name.description: Water body name for filtered lookup.

refid.description: Reference water body ID. More reliable than name matching.

coord.description: A coordinate within the target water body for geographic lookup.

anc_fields.description: Ancillary fields to include from ATL13 water body data.

field_selectors.label: Ancillary Field Selection
field_selectors.description: Request additional columns from the source HDF5 granules. Field names are HDF5 dataset path suffixes within the relevant subgroup. Adding fields increases processing time and response size. Each selector has its own applies_to — the group's applies_to is the union, not a default. A selector silently does nothing on an API not listed in its own applies_to.

atl03_ph_fields.description: Per-photon fields from the ATL03 /heights subgroup.
atl03_ph_fields.note: Only the atl03x endpoint reads ATL03 photon HDF5 groups. On any other X-series API this parameter is silently ignored.

atl03_geo_fields.description: Geolocation fields from the ATL03 /geolocation subgroup.
atl03_geo_fields.note: Only the atl03x endpoint reads ATL03 geolocation HDF5 groups. On any other X-series API this parameter is silently ignored.

atl03_corr_fields.description: Geophysical correction fields from the ATL03 /geophys_corr subgroup.
atl03_corr_fields.note: Only the atl03x endpoint reads ATL03 geophys_corr HDF5 groups. On any other X-series API this parameter is silently ignored.

atl03_bckgrd_fields.description: Background photon rate fields from the ATL03 /bckgrd_atlas subgroup.
atl03_bckgrd_fields.note: Only the atl03x endpoint reads ATL03 bckgrd_atlas HDF5 groups. On any other X-series API this parameter is silently ignored.

atl06_fields.description: Additional fields from ATL06 land-ice height segments.
atl06_fields.note: Only the atl06x endpoint reads the ATL06 land_ice_segments HDF5 group. On any other X-series API this parameter is silently ignored.

atl08_fields.description: Additional fields from ATL08 land-vegetation segments.
atl08_fields.implicit_behavior: On atl03x, atl08_fields requires either atl08_class or PhoREAL to already be active — setting it alone has no effect. It does NOT itself activate the ATL08 classifier; it appends ATL08 land_segments fields to output whose photons are already being classified by one of those other mechanisms. On atl08x, this prerequisite does not apply: the endpoint reads the native ATL08 land_segments HDF5 group directly, so atl08_fields appends columns unconditionally.
atl08_fields.note: On atl03x with PhoREAL extents, per-photon ATL08 values are reduced to per-extent by nearest neighbor (mode). Append '%' to a field name for interpolated (average) reduction instead. On atl08x there is no extent reduction — fields are appended one-to-one with land_segments rows.

atl09_fields.description: Atmospheric fields from ATL09 (cloud cover, atmospheric backscatter).
atl09_fields.note: Activates the Atl09Sampler runner on every X-series endpoint. ATL09 granules are looked up via CMR per the ATL03 RGT/cycle/region of each request.

atl13_fields.description: Additional fields from ATL13 inland water surface segments.
atl13_fields.note: Top-level atl13_fields is consumed only by the legacy Atl13Reader (atl13s/atl13sp endpoints), which are not part of this X-series schema. For atl13x, use the nested atl13.anc_fields parameter under the atl13 algorithm block instead.

asset.description: Source data asset identifier. Default 'icesat2' uses the standard ICESat-2 data holdings.
